clause_text: Data Collection: We collect your name, email, phone number, and contacts from your phone. We also track your precise GPS location at all times to improve our service.
audit_result:
The Company Clause is Non-Compliant with the DPDP Act 2023.

The clause states that the company collects the user's name, email, phone number, and contacts from their phone, and tracks their precise GPS location at all times to improve their service. However, it does not specify that the consent given by the Data Principal is free, specific, informed, unconditional, and unambiguous, as required by Section 6(1) of the DPDP Act.

Specifically, the clause does not:

* Obtain explicit consent for the collection of contacts from the user's phone, which may not be necessary for the specified purpose of improving the service.
* Provide an option for the Data Principal to access the contents of the notice in a language specified in the Eighth Schedule to the Constitution, as required by Section 3(3) of the DPDP Act.
* Limit the collection of personal data to what is necessary for the specified purpose, as required by Section 6(1) of the DPDP Act.

Suggested Correction:
The company should revise the clause to explicitly state that it obtains free, specific, informed, unconditional, and unambiguous consent from the user for the collection of their personal data, including contacts from their phone and GPS location. The company should also provide an option for the user to access the notice in a language of their choice and limit the collection of personal data to what is necessary for the specified purpose.

Revised Clause:
"We collect your name, email, and phone number with your explicit consent, which is free, specific, informed, unconditional, and unambiguous. We also request your consent to access your contacts from your phone, which is necessary for [ specify purpose, e.g., improving our service]. Additionally, we track your GPS location with your consent, which is necessary for [ specify purpose, e.g., providing location-based services]. You can access the contents of this notice in a language of your choice, as specified in the Eighth Schedule to the Constitution. We limit the collection of your personal data to what is necessary for the specified purpose and will not process it without your consent, as per Section 6(1) of the DPDP Act."

clause_text: Third-Party Sharing: By using our app, you agree that we may share your personal data with our marketing partners, insurance companies, and any third-party advertisers we choose, without further notice to you.
audit_result:
The Company Clause is Non-Compliant with the DPDP Act 2023.

The exact Section of the DPDP Act that is being infringed is Section 6(1), which states that consent given by the Data Principal shall be free, specific, informed, unconditional, and unambiguous with a clear affirmative action.

The Company Clause does not provide the Data Principal with specific, informed, and unambiguous consent, as it does not clearly state the purpose of sharing personal data with third-party advertisers and does not provide an option for the Data Principal to access the request in a language of their choice.

Suggested Correction: 
"By using our app, you agree that we may share your personal data with our marketing partners and insurance companies for the purpose of [ specify purpose, e.g., improving our services or providing personalized offers]. You also have the option to opt-out of sharing your personal data with third-party advertisers. Please note that you can access this request in English or any language specified in the Eighth Schedule to the Constitution. Our Data Protection Officer can be contacted at [provide contact details] for any queries or concerns regarding your personal data."

clause_text: Data Retention: We reserve the right to store your data indefinitely in our servers located in various international jurisdictions to ensure business continuity.
audit_result:
The Company Clause is Non-Compliant with the DPDP Act 2023. 

The exact Section of the DPDP Act that the Company Clause is non-compliant with is Section 8(7)(a), which states that a Data Fiduciary shall erase personal data upon the Data Principal withdrawing her consent or as soon as it is reasonable to assume that the specified purpose is no longer being served.

Suggested Correction: 
Data Retention: We will store your data for a period necessary to achieve the specified purpose, after which we will erase it, unless retention is necessary for compliance with any law for the time being in force or you have given consent for a longer retention period.

clause_text:
No Erasure: Due to the nature of our blockchain-based backend, we cannot delete or correct any data once it has been uploaded to our system.

Grievances: If you have any issues, you can email our general support team. We do not have a dedicated Data Protection Officer at this time.
audit_result:
The Company Clause is Non-Compliant with the DPDP Act 2023.

1. "No Erasure" clause: Non-Compliant with Section 12(3) of the DPDP Act, which states that a Data Principal has the right to erasure of her personal data. 
Suggested Correction: Implement a mechanism to erase personal data upon request from the Data Principal, unless retention is necessary for compliance with any law.

2. "Grievances" clause: Non-Compliant with Section 13(1) of the DPDP Act, which requires a Data Fiduciary to provide readily available means of grievance redressal. 
Suggested Correction: Establish a dedicated grievance redressal mechanism, including a Data Protection Officer, to address Data Principals' grievances.

3. "Legal Context" clause: Partially Compliant, as it mentions the responsibility of a Data Fiduciary, but it does not fully address the requirements of Section 8 of the DPDP Act.
Suggested Correction: Ensure that the company implements appropriate technical and organizational measures to comply with the provisions of the DPDP Act, including protecting personal data and responding to grievances.
